title: Motor Oil on Denim
timeEstimate: 30–90 minutes; powder + Goo Gone + Dawn cycles, indigo + ring-risk warnings, honest cap on aged
stainChemistry: Motor oil is heavier than cooking oil and harder to remove. Used motor oil is harder still because it carries engine contaminants: carbon particulate, metal particles, and dark petroleum compounds that add color along with the oily base. On denim, three complications stack: petroleum oil penetrates the heavy weave but the indigo dye sits on the surface and is fragile under solvent (Goo Gone, mineral spirits), aggressive scrubbing on dark denim leaves a halo ring, and used motor oil has a dark-color component (carbon + petroleum compounds) that may persist even after the oil is removed. The recurring traps: hot water (sets the petroleum residue), chlorine bleach (strips indigo unevenly), and skipping the hidden-seam test before solvent. Honest cap: aged set motor oil and used-oil residue often leave a dark shadow even after full sequence.
whyThisWorks: Powder preload (cornstarch / kitty litter / sawdust for heavier spills) absorbs bulk oil before water spreads it. Goo Gone (citrus solvent) on a cloth dissolves petroleum residue — like dissolves like. Dawn pretreat handles the residual oil after the solvent step. Cool wash plus AIR-DRY locks nothing. Multi-pass is normal: motor oil typically needs Goo Gone × 2 and Dawn × 2 minimum. The honest cap is real — used motor oil with carbon contamination often leaves a dark shadow no matter how many cycles.
scienceNote: Mechanism (pro-facing): motor oil is petroleum base oil + ZDDP additives + carbon particulate (used) + polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons. Pro uses POG / petroleum ether for petroleum phase + amyl acetate for polymerized residue + NSD + sodium hydrosulfite for residual carbon shadow.
defaultAssumption: Garage / driveway / home repair motor oil drip on jeans. Fresh = full sequence has a real shot. Used motor oil with carbon = honest cap (dark shadow may remain). Raw / selvage / black-overdyed denim = pro from minute one (any solvent + scrubbing risks visible color loss).

spottingProtocol.technique: Cat litter or cornstarch for bulk
spottingProtocol.equipment: Cornstarch / baking soda / clay-based kitty litter, plastic spoon
spottingProtocol.instruction: Cover the oil spot heavily with cornstarch, baking soda, or clay-based kitty litter (the cheap kind). For heavy oil, kitty litter absorbs faster. Press gently. 30–60 min absorb. Soft-brush off.
Inside seam: dab Goo Gone on hidden seam, blot with white cloth. Indigo transfers = skip Goo Gone, Dawn × 3 only. Light-wash denim usually passes; raw / dark / selvage often doesn't.

spottingProtocol.agent: Goo Gone Cloth Tamp — Petroleum Solvent
spottingProtocol.equipment: Goo Gone on white cotton cloth, ventilation
spottingProtocol.instruction: Apply Goo Gone to a clean white cloth — never pour on denim. Tamp the spot from the top, flip cloth as it picks up dark transfer. Outside-in. 2–3 passes. Open windows; flammable, no flame. STOP if the spot starts SPREADING outward — that's an indigo-bleed signal even if hidden test passed.
Generous Dawn directly on the spot. Soft toothbrush 10 min. Always blot, never rub — denim halos under aggressive scrubbing. Cool damp-cloth rinse.

spottingProtocol.agent: Repeat Goo Gone + Dawn If Oil Remains
spottingProtocol.technique: Motor oil typically needs multiple cycles
spottingProtocol.instruction: Inspect — oily feel or dark color remaining? Repeat Goo Gone + Dawn cycle. Motor oil typically needs Goo Gone × 2 and Dawn × 2 minimum.

Cool machine wash with normal detergent. AIR-DRY only. Inspect dry — wet denim hides residual that reappears once dry. NEVER hot dryer until cleared. Honest cap: used motor oil with carbon often leaves a dark shadow that won't fully clear.

steps: 1. Heavy powder preload 30–60 min (cornstarch / kitty litter for bulk).
2. Hidden-seam Goo Gone test on dark / raw denim.
3. Goo Gone cloth tamp for petroleum, outside-in, 2–3 passes. Stop if spreading.
4. Generous Dawn pretreat, cool, 10 min, soft toothbrush.
5. Repeat Goo Gone + Dawn if oil remains (motor oil typically multi-pass).
6. Cool machine wash + AIR-DRY. Honest cap on used oil with carbon.
products: Cornstarch / baking soda / clay-based kitty litter (heavy preload)
Goo Gone / citrus solvent (hidden seam test on indigo)
professionalProtocol.temperature: cool throughout — heat polymerizes motor oil into denim
warnings: NEVER hot water — polymerizes petroleum residue into denim
NEVER hot dryer until stain fully cleared
NEVER chlorine bleach on denim — strips indigo unevenly, leaves halo
Hidden-seam Goo Gone test on dark / raw denim
STOP if spot is SPREADING during Goo Gone — pro from there
Always blot, never rub — denim halos under scrubbing
Goo Gone is flammable — no flame, ventilate
Honest cap: used motor oil with carbon shadow often plateaus

homeSolutions.agent: Motor Oil on Denim Has an Honest Cap
homeSolutions.instruction: Motor oil is heavier than cooking oil and used motor oil carries dark carbon contamination that may not fully clear. Set realistic expectations from the start. Three hard rules: powder preload BEFORE water (water-first spreads petroleum into a wider ring), NO hot water (polymerizes petroleum into denim), and NO chlorine bleach (strips indigo unevenly on dark denim, leaves a halo). Multi-pass is normal — Goo Gone × 2 and Dawn × 2 is typical.

homeSolutions.agent: Heavy Powder Preload — Cornstarch or Kitty Litter
homeSolutions.instruction: Cover the oil spot heavily with cornstarch, baking soda, or clay-based kitty litter (the cheap unscented kind absorbs heavy petroleum faster than cornstarch). Press gently. 30 to 60 minutes absorb — longer for heavier spills. Soft-brush off. Most surface oil comes off this way.

Before any Goo Gone on dark or raw denim: dab a drop of Goo Gone on an inside seam, blot with a white cloth. If indigo transfers, skip Goo Gone — Dawn × 3 only. Light-wash denim usually passes; raw / dark / selvage / black-overdyed often doesn't.

homeSolutions.agent: Goo Gone for Petroleum — Cloth Tamp
homeSolutions.instruction: Apply Goo Gone (citrus solvent, orange bottle) to a clean white cloth — never pour on the denim. Tamp the spot from the top, flip the cloth as it picks up dark oil transfer. Outside-in. 2 to 3 passes. Open windows — Goo Gone is flammable, no flame nearby. STOP if the spot starts SPREADING outward — that's an indigo-bleed signal even if hidden test passed; pro from there.
Generous Dawn dish soap directly on the spot. Work in with a soft toothbrush for 10 minutes. Always blot, never rub — rubbing causes a halo on dark denim. Cool damp-cloth rinse.

homeSolutions.agent: Repeat If Oil Remains — Multi-Pass Is Normal
homeSolutions.instruction: Inspect the spot. Oily feel or dark color remaining? Repeat the Goo Gone + Dawn cycle. Motor oil typically needs Goo Gone × 2 and Dawn × 2 minimum to fully clear. Used motor oil with carbon contamination may leave a dark shadow regardless.

Cool machine wash with normal detergent. AIR-DRY only. Inspect dry — wet denim hides residual oil that reappears once dry. NEVER hot dryer until cleared. Honest cap: used motor oil with carbon contamination often leaves a dark shadow that won't fully clear at home — pro for valuable garments.

steps: 1. Set honest expectations — used motor oil with carbon often leaves a dark shadow.
2. NO hot water. NO chlorine bleach. Powder BEFORE water.
4. Hidden-seam Goo Gone test on dark / raw denim.
5. Goo Gone cloth tamp 2–3 passes — STOP if spreading.
7. Repeat Goo Gone + Dawn cycle (multi-pass is normal).
8. Cool machine wash + AIR-DRY. No dryer until cleared.
products: Cornstarch / baking soda / clay-based kitty litter
Goo Gone / citrus solvent (hidden seam test on indigo)
diyProtocol.whenToStop: Stop on raw / selvage / black-overdyed denim from minute one (pro). Stop if hidden-seam Goo Gone test shows indigo loss. Stop if spot SPREADS during Goo Gone application. Stop if hot water or hot dryer was already used (often permanent). Stop after multi-pass with used-oil carbon shadow remaining (honest cap).
warnings: NEVER hot water — polymerizes petroleum into denim
NEVER chlorine bleach on denim — strips indigo unevenly
Hidden-seam Goo Gone test on dark / raw denim
STOP if spreading — pro from there
Always blot, never rub — denim halos
Honest cap: used motor oil carbon shadow often plateaus

neverDo: Never hot water on motor oil + denim
Never rub denim during cleaning — causes halo ring
Never water-first on motor oil — spreads it wider
fiberSensitivities: Indigo / dark denim: hidden-seam Goo Gone test mandatory
Raw / selvage denim: pro from minute one — moisture causes uneven fading
Light-wash denim: most forgiving — full home protocol
Stretch denim with elastane: cool only — solvent can stress elastane
Black overdyed denim: pro — color-loss risk on solvent

materialWarnings: Motor oil on denim has an HONEST CAP — used oil with carbon often leaves dark shadow
Multi-pass is normal — Goo Gone × 2 and Dawn × 2 typical
Indigo dye loss is the central risk — hidden test before Goo Gone
Powder before water — water-first spreads petroleum
No hot water / no hot dryer — heat polymerizes motor oil
STOP if spreading — pro from there

escalation.when: Hot water or hot dryer already used (often permanent). Hidden-seam test shows indigo loss. Raw / selvage / black-overdyed denim from minute one. Spot SPREADING during Goo Gone (indigo bleed). Used-oil carbon shadow plateau after multi-pass. Heavy spill that soaked through to inside of garment.
escalation.whatToTell: "Motor oil on [light-wash / dark / indigo / raw / selvage] denim, [fresh / used oil / aged set], home-treated with [heavy cornstarch preload + Goo Gone × N + Dawn × N + cool wash + air-dry]. No hot, no chlorine. [Residual dark shadow / indigo loss / spreading observed / honest cap reached]."
escalation.specialistType: professional dry cleaner with denim experience plus dry-side solvent capability for petroleum (POG / amyl acetate)

contextQuestions: Fresh new oil or used oil with carbon?
Light-wash, dark indigo, raw / selvage, or black-overdyed denim?
Fresh, hours old, or hot-dried already?
How heavy was the spill (drops vs soaked)?
deepSolveHooks.modifierNotes: Used motor oil is much harder than fresh new oil because of carbon contamination — manage expectations. Heavy spills that soaked through to the inside of the garment usually need pro chemistry. Raw / selvage denim is pro from minute one because the dye is more fragile and any solvent risks visible color loss. Multi-pass (Goo Gone × 2, Dawn × 2) is the norm, not a failure mode.
